data.title: Orsted Shares Jump after U.S. Court Lifts Trump-Era Block on Key Wind Project
data.description:
Orsted (DOGEF) shares surged after a major U.S. court ruling cleared the Danish renewable energy giant to resume work on its nearly completed Revolution Wind project. The news lifted investor sentiment as it removed the threat of massive financial losses for the company.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The project, which is roughly 87–90% complete, had been halted twice in recent months. This created uncertainty for Orsted and its partner Skyborn Renewables, which have already invested billions in the development.

Shares of the company were up about 6% in early European trading.

Court Ruling Clears the Way for Orsted

A U.S. District Court judge ruled that construction on the $5 billion project could restart immediately, rejecting the Trump administration's attempt to pause offshore wind development over what it described as national‑security concerns.

The judge said the government's justification appeared insufficient and raised doubt about its real motives.

Orsted said it plans to restart construction "as soon as possible," emphasizing that the project will deliver affordable, reliable power to roughly 350,000 homes across Rhode Island and Connecticut once operational.

Ruling Lifts Outlook for Orsted's U.S. Projects amid Ongoing Policy Clash

The ruling also boosts sentiment about Orsted's broader U.S. portfolio. The decision raises hope for similar outcomes for other halted projects, including the company's larger Sunrise Wind development near New York, which is also tied up in legal challenges.

It is worth noting that the case is part of a wider clash between offshore wind developers and the Trump administration, which suspended five major wind leases late last year, citing Pentagon concerns about turbine interference with radar systems.

Importantly, courts have now twice rejected those arguments in Orsted's case, calling them insufficient to justify stopping work.

Are Orsted Shares a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, DOGEF has a Hold consensus rating based on three Buys, seven Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $21.62, the average Orsted price target implies 8.1% upside potential. Shares of the company have declined 16.85% over the past six months.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Rare Earth Stocks Jump after G7 Talks on Reducing Dependence on China
data.description:
American rare-earth and critical mineral producers' shares added to their Monday gains during pre-market trading on Tuesday. This follows a meeting of G7 finance ministers in Washington on Monday to reduce reliance on China's rare earth supply.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shares of the following companies rose:

  • MP Materials Corp. (MP) rose 5.39%, adding 3.80% to reach $67.82
  • USA Rare Earth (USAR) grew 2.66%, adding 1.58% to reach $18.02
  • Energy Fuels Inc. (UUUU) jumped 6.06%, adding 1.22% to reach $19.50
  • NioCorp Developments Limited (NB) climbed 9.52%, adding 1.16% to reach $6.98

"Throughout the course of discussions, attendees expressed a strong, shared desire to quickly address key vulnerabilities in critical minerals supply chains," the U.S. Department of the Treasury noted in a statement.

The meeting saw participation from the G7, including the EU, the UK, Canada, France, Germany, and Japan. They were also joined by non-G7 partners such as Australia, India, Mexico, and South Korea.

U.S.-Led Group Seeks Supply Chain Diversification

During the meeting, which was convened by the U.S. Secretary of the Treasury, Scott Bessent, the United States outlined the measures and investments it has already made, along with future plans "to create resilient, secure, and diversified critical minerals supply chains."

Rare earth and other critical minerals are central to the production of military defense systems, smartphones, and electric cars. However, while the G7 countries account for about 60% of global demand for these critical minerals, China refines between 47% and 87% of these minerals, according to the International Energy Agency.

China Leverages Supply Chain Control

As a result, China leverages its control in its trade conflict with the U.S. and recently saw its shipments of rare earth magnets to the U.S. drop sharply before export curbs were eased. This week, Beijing began restricting exports of rare earth magnets to Japan after it banned export of dual-use military items to the U.S. ally.

Over the past year, shares of U.S. and Australian critical mineral miners have rallied as the Trump administration advanced plans for a U.S. strategic mineral reserve and floated using price floors and forward purchases to support the market.

data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: BBC Battles Trump over Explosive $10 Billion Defamation Suit
data.description:
The British Broadcasting Corp. (BBC) just fired its first major legal shot against President Donald Trump's massive defamation suit. This fight started over an episode of the news program Panorama, where a speech from January 6, 2021, was edited in a way that made it look like the President was calling for violence. While the network admitted the edit was an "error of judgment," they are now telling a federal judge in Miami that a $10 billion penalty is a complete overreach.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The BBC Asks to Move the Case to Neutral Ground

The BBC is asking to have the case moved from Florida to New York. Their logic is that they didn't make the film in Florida, they didn't produce it there, and they didn't even air it there. By trying to exit the Florida court system, they hope to find a more neutral legal environment. They are also calling out the President's claim that the film was on the streaming service BritBox, stating that simply clicking the link proves it was never there.

The BBC Defends the Actual Malice Shield

The core of the BBC's defense rests on a tough legal standard called "actual malice." In the United States, a public figure like the President can only win a defamation case if they can prove the journalists intentionally lied or showed "reckless disregard" for the truth.

  • Admits the Error: The BBC chairman already apologized, which led to the resignations of Director-General Tim Davie and News CEO Deborah Turness.
  • Denies the Malice: Lawyers argue that a 15-second clip in a 60-minute film isn't proof of a "hit job." They claim that because the rest of the film was balanced, they didn't act with the "hatred or ill-will" required to lose a $10 billion case.

The BBC Aims to Block Private Document Leaks

Perhaps the most important part of this new defense is the attempt to pause discovery. In a normal lawsuit, both sides get to dig through each other's private emails and text messages. The BBC is fighting to stop this process because it would allow the President's team to see every internal conversation their journalists have had about him for the last ten years. This move effectively acts as a shield to keep their newsroom secrets private while they wait for a judge to decide if the case should even exist

Investors can track the latest on President Donald Trump on TipRanks' Trump News & Truth Social Market Impact Dashboard. Click on the image below to explore the tool.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: SK Hynix to Spend $12.9 Billion on AI Memory as Nvidia (NVDA) Demand Grows
data.description:
South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix announced it will invest $12.9 billion to build a new advanced chip packaging plant in South Korea. The project is aimed at meeting the rising demand for memory chips used in AI systems. Construction will begin in April, and the factory is expected to be ready by the end of next year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The focus of the new plant is high bandwidth memory, or HBM, which is used in AI data centers and servers. HBM stacks memory chips on top of each other to save space and lower power use. As a result, it has become a key part of modern AI hardware. SK Hynix said global AI competition is pushing memory demand higher, which makes early investment more important.

In the broader context, this move fits into a wider trend in South Korea to strengthen chip supply chains at home. Advanced packaging has become just as important as chip design, since AI chips depend on fast and efficient memory links. For SK Hynix, the investment helps secure both capacity and know-how in a tight market.

What This Means for Nvidia, Samsung, and Micron

SK Hynix is the main supplier of HBM to Nvidia (NVDA), which uses the memory in its AI accelerators. As Nvidia continues to ship more AI chips, a stable HBM supply becomes a key factor in meeting customer demand. Therefore, the new plant supports Nvidia's ability to scale production over time.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: GOOGL, AAPL: Wedbush's Dan Ives Sees Gemini Deal a Win for Both, Reaffirms Buy on Apple
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) has chosen Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google Gemini AI models to power its next-generation applications and models, including the Siri digital assistant. Wedbush's five-star-rated analyst Daniel Ives sees this recent deal as a win for both Google and Apple. To be precise, he added that the deal validates Google as a top AI model provider and gives Apple a boost to accelerate its AI plans into 2026 and beyond. On Tuesday, GOOGL shares rose 0.69% in premarket trading, while AAPL slipped 0.35%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Additionally, Ives stated that this is what investors have been waiting for, addressing the "elephant in the room" about Apple's hidden AI strategy. He sees the deal as a small but meaningful positive for both companies.

Apple Picks Google Gemini

For more context, Apple and Google have teamed up in a multi-year deal, under which Apple's next-generation Foundation Models will use Google's Gemini models and cloud technology. These models will support future AI features for Apple, including a more personalized Siri set to launch later this year.

The deal is a major vote of confidence for Google as the Siri partnership unlocks access to Apple's massive installed base of over two billion active devices. Notably, Google's AI progress has boosted its parent company, Alphabet, making it slightly more valuable than Apple in investors' eyes. On Monday, Alphabet briefly hit a $4 trillion market value in early trading before falling back below that level later in the session.

Wedbush Reaffirms Buy on AAPL Stock

Yesterday, Ives reaffirmed his Buy rating on AAPL with a price target of $350, forecasting nearly 35% upside. The stock remains on Wedbush's Best Ideas List and the Ives AI 30 List. Ives believes that 2026 will be a key year for Apple, with the launch of a new Siri, the Google Gemini partnership, and the iPhone 18 lineup.

He expects the partnership between Google Gemini and Apple Intelligence to be exclusive and pointed out that the DOJ case's conclusion against Google has cleared the way for its announcement. With this deal in place, Wedbush now sees 2026 as the year Apple must prove the value of its previously "invisible" AI efforts.

Is Apple a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, AAPL stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with 19 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sell assigned in the last three months. Apple's average stock price target is $299.69, suggesting a potential upside of 15.16% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Bernstein Analyst Names Amazon, Meta 'Top Large-Cap Tech Picks for 2026'
data.description:
Bernstein's top analyst Mark Shmulik named Amazon (AMZN) and Meta Platforms (META) as his top large-capitalization tech picks for 2026, after lagging the broader S&P 500 index (SPX) in 2025. For reference, AMZN stock rose 12.8% over the past year, while META gained only 5.5%, and the SPX surged about 19.4%. Both Magnificent 7 stocks are expected to outperform in 2026, with Amazon racing in the first half and Meta benefiting in the latter part of the year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shmulik reiterated his "Buy" rating on both stocks in a Monday client note outlining 2026 expectations. He emphasized that a central theme this year will be investors' focus pivoting from AI model performance to actual adoption of AI tools.

Shmulik is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #571 out of 10,398 analysts tracked. He has a 72% success rate and an average return per rating of 23.10%.

AMZN Stock: Project Rainier Will Drive AWS Growth in 2026

Shmulik expects Amazon to reverse the narrative that its Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud unit trails competitors in securing AI-related business. He attributed Amazon's lackluster 2025 stock returns to sluggish AWS growth relative to peers and flat retail operating income amid tariff challenges. At the same time, he acknowledged a lack of flashy product launches, partnerships, and compute deals.

For 2026, he expects AWS to accelerate after topping net new bookings in Q3 2025, with momentum building via Project Rainier. He believes that retail margins stand to improve steadily through cost-cutting layoffs, efficiency gains, and robotics adoption. Project Rainier is AWS's massive AI supercomputer, launched in late 2025, powered by nearly 500,000 Trainium2 chips across U.S. data centers for hyperscale AI training.

Shmulik kept his $300 price target on AMZN stock, which implies 21.7% upside potential from current levels.

META Stock: Most Compelling Mag 7 Catch-Up Trade for 2026

Shmulik highlighted that Meta has the highest overall upside among the stocks he covers, although it carries "notable risk." He noted that the stock is still rebounding from post-earnings sell-off after its Q3 report on October 29, when the social media giant surprised analysts by raising its 2026 capex forecast. Shmulik stated that Meta is pouring resources into data centers, aiming to develop AI-powered superintelligence.

According to Shmulik, Meta's aggressive push into frontier-model development triggered a major reset in 2025 spending, EPS, and free-cash-flow expectations. It's a "coin flip" whether the company can catch up to peers and deliver a best-in-class model early in 2026, he added. The bear case is that Meta's model benchmarks "close-but-not-quite" to peers, forcing endless investments over an undefined timeline just to close the gap.

However, Shmulik expects Meta's intense capex growth to peak this year. The tech giant has already proven it can grow and monetize AI products without leading in large language models (LLMs). "Meta is the most compelling Magnificent Seven catch-up trade for 2026 in our eyes," Shmulik wrote, reiterating his $870 price target, implying 35.5% upside potential.

AMZN or META: Which Is the Better Tech Stock?

The TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool shows that analysts have assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on both AMZN and META shares, with META offering a higher upside potential between the two.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Merck Sees $70B Revenue Potential From New Growth Drivers by Mid-2030s
data.description:
Pharma giant Merck (MRK) is targeting $70 billion in revenue from new growth drivers by the mid-2030s, as the company accelerates the launch of new drugs to mitigate the impact of the loss of business from the patent expiration of its blockbuster cancer drug Keytruda in 2028. This new target marks a step up from the company's previously announced estimate of $50 billion in new growth opportunities. Merck discussed its new target and growth drivers at the 44th annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference held on Monday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Merck Seeks to Boost Business via Organic Growth and Strategic Deals

During the event, Merck's CEO Robert Davis expressed confidence that the company would achieve $50 billion of its $70 billion target by the early 2030s. Davis added that the company's optimism is based on 10 major programs that make up 70% of the $70 billion estimate. Merck views all these programs as multibillion-dollar opportunities. In fact, four of the 10 programs are either already launched or have positive Phase III data, with the remaining expected to have important Phase III readouts over the next 12 to 18 months. 

According to Reuters, Merck expects cardiometabolic and respiratory treatments to contribute nearly $20 billion in sales, up from its previous ⁠forecast of $15 ​billion. Also, infectious disease drugs are estimated to generate about $15 billion, compared with the earlier projection of $5 billion.

Additionally, the company is open to further strategic acquisitions. According to Bloomberg, CEO Davis said, "We are not limited from a balance sheet — it's more where do we see strategic opportunity."

The CEO's comments come amid reports that Merck is in talks to buy cancer drugmaker Revolution Medicines (RVMD). Citing sources, the Financial Times reported last week that Merck is considering acquiring Revolution Medicines for $28 billion to $32 billion.   

Is Merck Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Merck stock based on 11 Buys and five Holds. The average MRK stock price target of $119.53 indicates 9.5% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: BlackRock (BLK) Shifts AI Focus from Big Tech to Energy and Infrastructure Stocks
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK), the world's largest asset manager, still sees AI as one of the strongest forces shaping markets in 2026. Yet the firm is turning its attention toward the sectors that keep this technology running. In its latest Investment Directions report, BlackRock wrote that it will focus more on energy and infrastructure providers rather than the big technology names that led markets through 2025.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to the firm's survey of 732 clients across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, only about one-fifth of investors still view the largest U.S. tech groups, such as Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), and Alphabet (GOOGL), as the most attractive AI opportunities. More than half of respondents preferred energy companies that power the fast-growing network of data centers supporting AI systems. Companies such as NextEra Energy (NEE), Vistra Corp. (VST), or Talen Energy (TLN) are considered potential stocks in this sector.

Meanwhile, 37% said they favored infrastructure builders as their top AI investment pick, with companies such as Quanta Services (PWR) and Vertiv Holdings (VRT) coming to mind.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Updates 2026 Model Y with Third Row Seating and Sleeker Interior
data.description:
Tesla's (TSLA) popular Model Y is getting a new look for 2026, and the key upgrade is the return of the third‑row seating option in the U.S. The seven-seat setup is now back on the EV giant's online configurator, making it available again for the first time since it was removed in earlier updates.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The new third row is offered exclusively on the Premium All-Wheel-Drive Long Range variant and costs an additional $2,500. As before, the seats are compact and best suited for children, with limited leg and headroom, a design choice that keeps Model Y's footprint unchanged while still boosting passenger capacity.

Beyond seating, Tesla has rolled out a handful of design and tech upgrades for the 2026 model. Premium trims now feature a black headliner, a darker 20-inch Helix wheel option, and a new 16-inch high‑resolution center display, previously seen in China on the Model Y Performance and Model YL.

2026 Model Y to Help TSLA Stay Competitive

The return of the third row is helpful for buyers who need extra seats occasionally without upgrading to a bigger SUV. While it is not a full replacement for a true three-row model, the new model adds flexibility for families with small kids.

While Tesla's 2026 Model Y refresh is not a dramatic overhaul, the combination of added seating, upgraded tech, and cleaner interior styling helps keep the company's most popular SUV competitive in an increasingly crowded EV market.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. At $401.93, the average Tesla price target implies a 10.47% downside risk. The stock has gained 41.67% over the past six months.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Slip Ahead of CPI Data, Big Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down early Tuesday as traders anticipated key Consumer Price Index (CPI) data and big bank results for Q4. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.23%, 0.14%, and 0.12%, respectively, at 3:45 a.m. EST on January 13.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Monday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed in the green, with the S&P 500 rising 0.16%, the Dow gaining 0.17%, and the Nasdaq Composite advancing 0.26%.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch today's CPI data to gain greater understanding of inflationary trends and fill gaps from last fall's extended U.S. government shutdown. Dow Jones consensus estimates anticipate a 2.7% price rise over the twelve months through December. This figure aligns with November's lower-than-expected CPI results.

Moreover, banking giant JPMorgan (JPM) will kick off the Q4 earnings season today, joined by airline leader Delta Air Lines (DAL).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.19%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $59.82 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,589 per ounce on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed amid persistent geopolitical tensions involving Iran and Greenland. U.S. President Donald Trump stated last night that any country doing business with Iran will face a 25% tariff on all their business with the U.S.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on January 13 amid Iran's ongoing geopolitical unrest.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.90%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.64%, while the Shenzhen Component added 1.44%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei surged 3.10%, and the Topix added 2.41%.
